narrative.headline: 2022 Toyota Highlander: power train leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 215 complaints with NHTSA. The power train is the most reported area at 20%, followed by the structure (18%) and service brakes (16%). The brakes carry the only reported death, and the air bags stand out for serious outcomes, with 7 crashes and 8 injuries from just 21 reports. Both are worth a close look before buying.

issues.sample: Highlander with approximately 650 miles on it had a transmission failure while on interstate 69 north of Evansville Indiana.  The car seemed to shift into passing gear a few times while the cruise was set and we bumped it off cruise and coasted to a stop . The car would not move
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 42 reports (20%), with 1 crash and 1 fire. Watch for transmission or shifting trouble, including loss of power while driving.

issues.title: Structure
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 39 reports (18%), linked to 1 crash and 4 injuries. Reports include body parts coming loose at highway speed.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: 35 reports (16%), with 3 crashes and the only reported death in this data. Test the brakes carefully and watch for them briefly letting go when stopping.

issues.title: Air Bags
issues.blurb: Only 21 reports (10%) but the most serious by outcome: 7 crashes and 8 injuries. Reports include the airbag warning light staying on. Worth checking closely.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: The smallest area here: 16 reports (7%), including 1 fire. Reports include sluggish response when trying to accelerate.
